summary: McKenzie explains that PayPal does allow customers to purchase without a PayPal account, despite widespread belief to the contrary among even experienced sellers. He walks through how PayPal's checkout flow presents different interfaces based on whether the customer has a PayPal cookie. He then explains the financial incentives behind this design: PayPal maximizes profit by steering existing users toward balance or ACH payments (which cost less than credit card interchange fees) while still allowing guest checkout to avoid losing conversions from non-members.
key_points: PayPal guest checkout without an account is possible and well-supported, despite being poorly documented and widely misunderstood by sellers
PayPal's checkout flow is cookie-based: cookied users see a login prompt, while non-cookied users see a split screen with guest checkout and sign-in options
PayPal's revenue model depends on interchange fees, and they profit most when users pay via PayPal balance (free) or ACH (cheap) rather than credit cards
The guest checkout exists because requiring PayPal accounts would cost conversions, which costs PayPal revenue
money_quotes: This fact isn't very well documented and I've seen folks who have sold $100,000+ with Paypal tell you it is impossible.
You have to understand that Paypal's primary source of revenue is interchange fees. And their primary expense? Also interchange fees.
If, however, that customer had a Paypal balance and paid through Paypal, completing the transaction is free to them — all they have to do is a database update on their own records.
Conversions are much higher when all you need to do is convince people to signin and click 'Purchase' rather than filling out a dozen fields in a form.
For those folks who are holdouts about getting a Paypal account, requiring them to get one costs conversions and thus costs Paypal money.
Paypal's check out experience is optimized to make them money, but as a side effect that means it is also optimized to make you money.
key_insight: PayPal's checkout flow is engineered around their interchange fee economics, which incidentally means it's also optimized to maximize conversions for sellers by automatically presenting the right purchasing option to each customer.
